generation_modes.parametric: AI picks parameter values from declared ranges. Backend constructs question text and computes answer.
generation_modes.curated_bank: Answers come from a hardcoded lookup table. AI selects a question_key from the bank.

distractor_strategies.PARTIAL: result of stopping one step early
distractor_strategies.FACTOR_SKIP: missing a factor or wrong exponent
distractor_strategies.FORMULA_MIX: result of a related but wrong formula
distractor_strategies.CURATED_WRONG: hand-picked plausible wrong answers (for curated_bank mode)

templates.topic: Square roots of perfect squares
templates.question_template: What is √{n}?
context_variants: Evaluate √{n}.
What is the square root of {n}?

templates.topic: Express as product of prime factors in index form
templates.question_template: Express {n} as a product of prime factors in index form.
context_variants: Write {n} as a product of its prime factors.
Prime factorise {n}.

templates.topic: Order rational numbers (fractions, decimals, mixed)
templates.question_template: Which list is in ascending order?
templates.bank_note: Each entry: 4 options, each a list of 3 numbers. One option is correct ascending order.

templates.topic: Round decimals to specified decimal places
templates.question_template: Round {x} to {dp} decimal place(s).
context_variants: What is {x} rounded to {dp} decimal place(s)?
templates.generation_constraint: x must have non-zero digit at dp+1 position to make rounding non-trivial

templates.topic: Multiply and divide fractions
templates.question_template: Calculate {a}/{b} {op} {c}/{d}. Express in simplest form.
context_variants: What is {a}/{b} {op} {c}/{d}?
Evaluate {a}/{b} {op} {c}/{d}.
templates.generation_constraint: result denominator ≤ 30 after simplification; result must not simplify to integer for interesting MCQ

templates.topic: Order of operations with positive rational numbers
templates.question_template: Evaluate: {expr}

expr.foundation: a op b op c where a,b,c ∈ [1..20], op ∈ {+,-,×}; no brackets; result positive integer
expr.standard: a op (b op c) where brackets change the result; result positive integer
expr.advanced: a × b + c ÷ d; a,b,c,d chosen so result is positive integer

templates.generation_constraint: expressions must have unambiguous integer answer; no division resulting in non-terminating decimal

templates.topic: Percentage of amount; express one quantity as percentage of another
templates.question_template: What is {pct}% of {amount}?
context_variants: Calculate {pct}% of {amount}.
{amount} students are in a school. {pct}% play sport. How many is that?
templates.generation_constraint: pct × amount must be divisible by 100 to yield integer answer

templates.topic: Add and subtract directed numbers (integers)
templates.question_template: Calculate {a} {op} {b}.
context_variants: Evaluate {a} {op} {b}.
What is {a} {op} {b}?
templates.generation_constraint: result ∈ [-100, 100]; for advanced ×, |a|,|b| ≤ 12

templates.topic: Divide a quantity in a given ratio
templates.question_template: Share {total} in the ratio {a}:{b}. What is the larger share?
context_variants: Divide ${total} between two people in the ratio {a}:{b}. How much does the person with the larger share receive?
A recipe uses flour and sugar in the ratio {a}:{b}. If {total}g is used in total, how much flour is there?
templates.generation_constraint: a ≠ b; total divisible by (a+b); result is positive integer

templates.topic: Substitute a value into a linear expression
templates.question_template: If {var} = {val}, what is the value of {expr}?

templates.topic: Solve one-step and two-step linear equations
templates.question_template: Solve: {a}{var} {op} {b} = {c}
context_variants: Find {var}: {a}{var} {op} {b} = {c}
What value of {var} makes {a}{var} {op} {b} = {c} true?
templates.generation_constraint: a=1 for foundation; solution is always an integer

templates.topic: Continue arithmetic sequence; identify common difference
templates.question_template: A sequence starts: {t1}, {t2}, {t3}, {t4}, … What is the next term?
context_variants: What is the 5th term of the sequence {t1}, {t2}, {t3}, {t4}, …?
Find the common difference of the sequence {t1}, {t2}, {t3}, {t4}.

templates.topic: Area of triangles and parallelograms
templates.question_template: A {shape} has base {b} cm and perpendicular height {h} cm. What is its area?
templates.generation_constraint: for triangle: b×h divisible by 2; for parallelogram: b×h ≤ 400

templates.topic: Volume of rectangular prisms
templates.question_template: A rectangular prism has length {l} cm, width {w} cm, and height {h} cm. What is its volume?

templates.topic: Unit conversions (length, mass, capacity)
templates.question_template: Convert {value} {from_unit} to {to_unit}.
templates.bank_note: Bank covers: km↔m, m↔cm, cm↔mm, kg↔g, L↔mL, and combinations. All results clean integers or 1dp.

templates.topic: Angle relationships when parallel lines are cut by a transversal
templates.question_template: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. One angle is {a}°. The marked angle is {relationship} to this angle. What is the size of the marked angle?
context_variants: Parallel lines are cut by a transversal. An angle of {a}° is formed. What is the size of the {relationship} angle?
Lines AB and CD are parallel. A transversal crosses both. One angle measures {a}°. What is the {relationship} angle?
templates.generation_constraint: a ∈ [35..85] for foundation/standard; any value for advanced. Result always positive integer.

templates._note: Supplementary/complementary/vertically opposite — originally misassigned to T-7M-04. Now correctly associated with VC2M7M05 elaboration content. Kept because it is high-frequency drill content.
templates.topic: Supplementary, complementary, and vertically opposite angles
templates.question_template: Two angles are {relationship}. One angle is {a}°. What is the other angle?
context_variants: Angle ABC = {a}°. Angle ABC and angle DEF are {relationship}. What is angle DEF?
An angle measures {a}°. What is its {relationship_noun}?

templates.topic: Interior angle sum of triangle; find unknown angle
templates.question_template: A triangle has angles of {a}° and {b}°. What is the size of the third angle?
context_variants: In triangle ABC, angle A = {a}° and angle B = {b}°. What is angle C?
Two angles of a triangle measure {a}° and {b}°. Find the third angle.
A triangle has one angle of {a}° and another of {b}°. What is the remaining angle?

b.rule: b chosen so a + b < 180 and third angle c = 180 - a - b ∈ [15, 145]
b.foundation_range: a + b ∈ [60, 120] so c is always reasonable
b.advanced_range: a + b ∈ [30, 165], includes very obtuse triangles

templates.distractor_notes: distractors: 180-(a+b)+5, 360-(a+b), a+b (student adds instead of subtracts from 180)
templates.generation_constraint: a + b < 180; third angle = 180 - a - b must be ≥ 15 and ≤ 150; all three angles must sum to exactly 180

templates.topic: Classify triangles and quadrilaterals by properties
templates.question_template: A triangle has {property_description}. What type of triangle is it?
templates.bank_note: Covers: equilateral, isosceles, scalene, right-angled, obtuse, acute; also square, rectangle, rhombus, parallelogram, trapezium, kite.

templates.topic: Find image coordinates after a single transformation
templates.question_template: Point {label}({x},{y}) is {transformation}. What are the coordinates of the image?

templates.topic: Read coordinates from Cartesian plane; identify quadrant
templates.question_template: Which point has coordinates ({x},{y})?
templates.bank_note: Diagram described textually with 4 labelled points. Student identifies which label matches the coordinate.

templates.topic: Calculate mean, median, mode, range from a data set
templates.question_template: The data set is: {data}. What is the {measure}?

data.foundation: 5–6 small integers ∈ [1..20], at least one repeated value
data.standard: 7–8 integers ∈ [1..50], may have two modes or no mode
data.advanced: 8–10 integers ∈ [1..100], even count for non-integer median

templates.generation_constraint: mean must be clean integer or 1dp; median of even list must be 0.5-step

templates.topic: Choose appropriate graph type for a described data situation
templates.question_template: A student wants to display {scenario}. Which type of graph is most appropriate?
templates.bank_note: Scenarios map to: column/bar chart (categories), line graph (change over time), pie chart (parts of a whole), scatter plot (relationship between two variables), histogram (continuous grouped data).

templates.topic: Assign probability as a fraction to a simple event
templates.question_template: A bag contains {r} red and {b} blue marbles. What is the probability of picking a red marble?
context_variants: A die has {n} sides numbered 1 to {n}. What is the probability of rolling a {target}?
A spinner has {total} equal sections, {favourable} of which are green. What is the probability of landing on green?
templates.generation_constraint: total = r+b ≤ 12; fraction already in simplest form OR simplification is obvious

templates.topic: List the sample space for a simple single-stage event
templates.question_template: A fair coin is flipped and a {die_type} die is rolled. How many outcomes are in the sample space?
templates.bank_note: Covers: coins, dice (4, 6, 8 sides), spinners with n sections, drawing from a small set.

templates.topic: Complementary events: P(A') = 1 - P(A)
templates.question_template: The probability of event A is {p}. What is the probability of A NOT occurring?
context_variants: P(rain tomorrow) = {p}. What is P(no rain tomorrow)?
The probability of winning a game is {p}. What is the probability of NOT winning?

templates.topic: Evaluate integer powers and roots; classify as rational/irrational
templates.question_template: Evaluate {expr}.

templates.topic: Recognise irrational numbers including π and square roots of non-perfect-squares
templates.question_template: Which of the following is an irrational number?
templates.bank_note: 3 options are rational (including traps: √4, 22/7, 0.333..., terminating decimals), 1 is irrational (√2, √3, √5, π, √50 etc). Exploit common misconceptions at Year 8 level.

templates.topic: Apply index laws: product, quotient, zero exponent
templates.question_template: Simplify: {expr}
templates.generation_constraint: for quotient: m > n; answer exponent ∈ [0..9]

templates.topic: Highest Common Factor and Lowest Common Multiple
templates.question_template: What is the {measure} of {a} and {b}?
templates.generation_constraint: gcd(a,b) > 1 to make HCF non-trivial; LCM ≤ 300

templates.topic: Operations with integers and rational numbers (all four operations)
templates.question_template: Calculate: {expr}

expr.foundation: integer × integer, integer ÷ integer (clean result), int + frac
expr.standard: frac op frac op int, mixed ops including ÷
expr.advanced: mixed operations with negatives: (-a/b) op (c/d) op e

templates.generation_constraint: result denominator ≤ 20 after simplification; no surds

templates.topic: Percentage increase, decrease, and error
templates.question_template: A price of ${original} is {change_type} by {pct}%. What is the new price?
context_variants: A value of {original} increases by {pct}%. What is the new value?
An item originally costs ${original}. It is discounted by {pct}%. What is the sale price?
templates.generation_constraint: result must be clean integer or 2dp decimal

templates.topic: Expand using distributive law; simplify algebraic expressions
templates.question_template: Expand and simplify: {expr}

templates.topic: Solve linear equations including with pronumerals on both sides
templates.question_template: Solve: {a}x {op1} {b} = {c}x {op2} {d}

templates.topic: Gradient from two points; identify gradient and y-intercept from y=mx+c
templates.question_template: What is the gradient of the line passing through ({x1},{y1}) and ({x2},{y2})?
context_variants: A line has equation y = {m}x + {c}. What is its gradient?
A line has equation y = {m}x + {c}. What is its y-intercept?

m.rule: m = rise / (x2 - x1), must be integer or simple fraction

templates.generation_constraint: gradient must be integer or fraction with denominator ≤ 4

templates.topic: Area of composite shapes
templates.question_template: Find the area of the composite shape formed by {description}.
templates.bank_note: Bank uses L-shapes, shapes with a rectangle and triangle combined. All dimensions given; integer answers only.

templates.topic: Volume of prisms with various cross-sections
templates.question_template: A {prism_type} prism has a {base_shape} cross-section with {base_dims}. Its length is {l} cm. What is its volume?

params.base_dims_and_area: generated to give integer cross-sectional area

templates.topic: Circumference and area of circles
templates.question_template: A circle has {dimension_type} {value} cm. Find its {measure}. Use π ≈ 3.14. Give your answer to 2 decimal places.

templates.topic: Calculate time duration and convert across time zones
templates.question_template: It is {time} in {city1}. What time is it in {city2}?
templates.bank_note: Uses AEST, AEDT, UTC, London, New York, Los Angeles. Offsets are whole hours. No DST complications unless advanced.

templates.topic: Rate problems: speed, unit rate, best buy
templates.question_template: A car travels {dist} km in {time} hours. What is its average speed?
context_variants: If speed is {speed} km/h, how far does a car travel in {time} hours?
A car travels {dist} km at {speed} km/h. How long does the journey take?

templates.topic: Pythagoras' theorem: find unknown side of right triangle
templates.question_template: A right triangle has {known_description}. Find the {unknown_side}.

templates.topic: Identify congruence condition from given information (SSS, SAS, AAS, RHS)
templates.question_template: Two triangles have {given_info}. Which congruence condition proves they are congruent?
templates.bank_note: 4 options always: SSS, SAS, AAS, RHS. One is correct for the described scenario.

templates.topic: Properties of quadrilaterals (angles, sides, diagonals)
templates.question_template: Which quadrilateral has {property_description}?
templates.bank_note: Covers: square, rectangle, rhombus, parallelogram, trapezium, kite. Properties tested: equal sides, parallel sides, equal angles, bisecting diagonals, perpendicular diagonals.

templates.topic: Find coordinates after combined transformations
templates.question_template: Point ({x},{y}) is {transformation}. What are the new coordinates?

templates.topic: Calculate mean from a frequency table
templates.question_template: The frequency table shows: {table_description}. What is the mean?

params.frequencies: list of corresponding frequencies ∈ [1..8]; total n ∈ [10..30]

templates.generation_constraint: mean is not a whole number to distinguish from mode

templates.topic: Effect of an outlier on mean vs median
templates.question_template: A data set is {data}. An outlier of {outlier} is added. Which statement is true?
templates.bank_note: Options always cover: mean increases/decreases more than median; median unaffected; both equally affected.

templates.topic: Complementary events: P(A) + P(not A) = 1
templates.question_template: The probability of event A occurring is {p}. What is the probability of event A NOT occurring?
context_variants: The probability that it rains tomorrow is {p}. What is the probability that it does NOT rain tomorrow?
A student has a {p} probability of winning a game. What is the probability of NOT winning?
The probability of drawing a red card from a deck is {p}. What is the probability of NOT drawing a red card?
templates.distractor_notes: wrong answers: p itself (forgot to subtract), 1+p (added instead), p/2 (halved), 1-(p+1/denom) (off by one on numerator)

templates.topic: Probability from two-step experiments using tree diagrams
templates.question_template: A bag has {r} red and {b} blue balls. One ball is drawn, then replaced, and another is drawn. What is the probability of drawing {outcome}?
templates.generation_constraint: r+b ≤ 6; answer denominator ≤ 36

templates.topic: Relative frequency from a frequency table
templates.question_template: In {n} trials, outcome A occurred {a} times. What is the relative frequency of A?

templates.topic: Classify real numbers as rational or irrational
templates.question_template: Which of the following is an irrational number?
templates.bank_note: Options include: √4 (rational), √2 (irrational), 0.333... (rational), π (irrational), 22/7 (rational), 1.41421... (irrational if non-terminating non-recurring).

templates.topic: Scientific notation: convert to/from standard form; multiply/divide
templates.question_template: Express {value} in scientific notation.
context_variants: Write {sci_value} as an ordinary number.
Calculate ({a} × 10^{m}) × ({b} × 10^{n}). Express in scientific notation.

value.advanced: any value requiring 2 or 3 significant figures

templates.topic: Simple and compound interest
templates.question_template: Calculate the {interest_type} interest on ${principal} at {rate}% per annum for {years} years.
context_variants: What is the total amount after {years} years on a ${principal} investment at {rate}% p.a. compound interest?

templates.topic: Direct and inverse proportion
templates.question_template: If {y} is directly proportional to {x}, and {y}={y1} when {x}={x1}, find {y} when {x}={x2}.

templates.topic: Expand and simplify binomial products; factorise simple expressions
templates.question_template: Expand and simplify: ({ax} {op1} {b})({cx} {op2} {d})
context_variants: Factorise: {expanded_form}
Expand: ({ax} + {b})²
templates.generation_constraint: all coefficients must be integers; result has form ax²+bx+c

templates.topic: Solve linear equations including with fractions
templates.question_template: Solve: {lhs} = {rhs}

templates.topic: Equation of a straight line; gradient and intercept from y=mx+c
templates.question_template: A line has gradient {m} and y-intercept {c}. What is the equation of the line?
context_variants: What is the gradient of the line y = {m}x + {c}?
What is the y-intercept of the line y = {m}x + {c}?
A line passes through ({x1},{y1}) with gradient {m}. What is its equation?

templates.topic: Solve simultaneous linear equations by substitution
context_variants: Find the intersection point of y = {a}x + {b} and y = {c}x + {d}.

templates.topic: Midpoint of a line segment between two points on the Cartesian plane
templates.question_template: What is the midpoint of the line segment joining ({x1},{y1}) and ({x2},{y2})?
context_variants: Find the midpoint of the interval from A({x1},{y1}) to B({x2},{y2}).
Points P({x1},{y1}) and Q({x2},{y2}) are the endpoints of a line segment. What are the coordinates of the midpoint?

x2.rule: x2 = x1 + 2k where k ∈ [-4, 4]\{0}, ensuring midpoint x = x1+k is integer

y2.rule: y2 = y1 + 2j where j ∈ [-4, 4]\{0}, ensuring midpoint y = y1+j is integer

templates.distractor_notes: distractors: (x2-x1, y2-y1) distance not midpoint; (x1+x2, y1+y2) forgot to divide by 2; midpoint shifted by ±1 on one coordinate
templates.generation_constraint: midpoint coordinates must both be integers — use only even differences between x1,x2 and y1,y2

templates.topic: Distance between two points on the Cartesian plane
templates.question_template: What is the distance between the points ({x1},{y1}) and ({x2},{y2})?
context_variants: Find the length of the line segment from A({x1},{y1}) to B({x2},{y2}).
Calculate the distance between P({x1},{y1}) and Q({x2},{y2}). Give your answer as an exact value or to 2 decimal places.

pythagorean_triple.note: use Pythagorean triples to guarantee integer distances; scale by 1 or 2

templates.distractor_notes: distractors: Δx + Δy (Manhattan distance), (Δx)²+(Δy)² (forgot square root), (Δx)+(Δy) just adding, distance±1
templates.generation_constraint: always use Pythagorean triples scaled so distance is a clean integer; avoids surd answers that make MCQ ambiguous

templates.topic: Classify non-linear relationships from equation or graph shape
templates.question_template: Which equation represents a {relationship_type} relationship?
templates.bank_note: Types: linear (y=mx+c), quadratic (y=ax²+bx+c), hyperbola (y=k/x), exponential (y=a^x). Options are 4 different equations.

templates.topic: Surface area of prisms and cylinders
templates.question_template: A {shape} has {dimensions}. Find its surface area. Use π ≈ 3.14 where needed.

params.dimensions: generated to give integer or 2dp answer

templates.topic: Volume of pyramids, cones, and spheres
templates.question_template: A {shape} has {dimensions}. Find its volume. Use π ≈ 3.14 where needed.

dimensions.pyramid: base side {s} cm, height {h} cm; s ∈ [3..12], h ∈ [4..15]
dimensions.cone: radius {r} cm, height {h} cm; r ∈ [3..9], h ∈ [4..15]

templates.topic: Trigonometry: find an unknown side using sin, cos, or tan
templates.question_template: In a right-angled triangle, angle θ = {theta}° and the {known_side} = {value} cm. Find the {unknown_side} (to 2 dp).

templates.topic: Calculate percentage error between measured and actual values
templates.question_template: A measurement is recorded as {measured}. The actual value is {actual}. What is the percentage error?
context_variants: A student measures a length as {measured} cm. The exact length is {actual} cm. Calculate the percentage error.
An estimate of {measured} is made for a quantity whose true value is {actual}. What is the percentage error (to 1 decimal place)?

measured.rule: measured = actual × (1 + error_pct/100) if over, or actual × (1 - error_pct/100) if under

templates.distractor_notes: distractors: absolute error not percentage, (measured-actual)/measured × 100 (wrong denominator), error×2, error/2
templates.generation_constraint: measured must be a clean integer (use only error_pct values that make actual × pct/100 an integer); answer is always a clean percentage like 5.0% or 10.0%

templates.topic: Find missing length in similar figures
templates.question_template: Two similar triangles have corresponding sides {a} cm and {b} cm. Another side of the smaller triangle is {c} cm. What is the corresponding side of the larger triangle?
templates.generation_constraint: answer is integer or clean 1dp decimal

templates.topic: Compare summary statistics between two data sets
templates.question_template: Data set A has mean {meanA} and range {rangeA}. Data set B has mean {meanB} and range {rangeB}. Which statement is true?
templates.bank_note: Options are always: 4 statements comparing the two sets. Exactly one is factually correct based on the given params.

templates.topic: Venn diagrams: union, intersection, complement
templates.question_template: In a group of {total} students, {a} study Maths, {b} study Science, and {ab} study both. What is the probability that a randomly chosen student studies {query}?

templates.topic: Two-way tables: read frequencies and calculate probability
templates.question_template: A two-way table shows: {table_description}. What is the probability that a randomly selected person {query}?

params.table_structure: 2×2 table: two categories (e.g. Gender × Preference), each cell integer ∈ [5..30]

standard: is in a specific row or column
is in one category given another
advanced: conditional probability given a row or column

curated_banks._note: These banks must be populated before the system goes live. Each bank is a list of items. Each item has a question_key, question_params, correct_answer, and three wrong_answers.

rational_ordering_bank.question_text: Which list is in ascending order?
Which list is in ascending order?
Which list is in ascending order?
Which list is in ascending order?
Which list is in ascending order?
Which list is in ascending order?
Which list is in ascending order?
Which list is in ascending order?
Which list is in ascending order?
Which list is in ascending order?
Which list is in ascending order?
Which list is in ascending order?
Which list is in ascending order?
Which list is in ascending order?
Which list is in ascending order?
Which list is in ascending order?
Which list is in ascending order?
Which list is in ascending order?
Which list is in ascending order?
Which list is in ascending order?
Which list is in ascending order?
Which list is in ascending order?
Which list is in ascending order?
Which list is in ascending order?
Which list is in ascending order?
Which list is in ascending order?
Which list is in ascending order?
Which list is in ascending order?
Which list is in ascending order?
Which list is in ascending order?

unit_conversion_bank.question_text: Convert 0.4 km to metres, then to centimetres. What is the answer in centimetres?
A container holds 4.5 L. How many millilitres is this? Then convert to litres and add 500 mL. What is the total in millilitres?

shape_classification_bank.question_text: A triangle has side lengths 6 cm, 6 cm and 6 cm. What type of triangle is it?
A triangle has side lengths 5 cm, 5 cm and 7 cm. What type of triangle is it?
A triangle has side lengths 4 cm, 6 cm and 9 cm. What type of triangle is it?
A triangle has sides of length 3 cm, 4 cm and 5 cm. Classify this triangle by its angles.
A triangle has angles of 60°, 70° and 50°. Classify this triangle by its angles.
A triangle has angles of 25°, 35° and 120°. Classify this triangle by its angles.
A triangle has two angles that each measure 70°. What type of triangle is it, classified by its sides?
A triangle has sides of length 5 cm, 12 cm and 13 cm. Classify this triangle by its angles.
A triangle has side lengths 3 cm, 5 cm and 7 cm. Given that 3² + 5² < 7², classify this triangle by its angles.
A triangle has three angles that each measure 60°. What type of triangle is it, classified by its sides?
A quadrilateral has all four sides equal in length and all four angles equal to 90°. What type of quadrilateral is it?
A quadrilateral has two pairs of equal opposite sides, all angles are 90°, but not all four sides are the same length. What type of quadrilateral is it?
A quadrilateral has all four sides equal in length but its angles are not 90°. What type of quadrilateral is it?
A quadrilateral has two pairs of parallel sides. Opposite sides are equal but not all sides are equal. Its angles are not 90°. What type of quadrilateral is it?
A quadrilateral has exactly one pair of parallel sides. What type of quadrilateral is it?
A quadrilateral has two pairs of equal adjacent sides, but its opposite sides are not equal or parallel. What type of quadrilateral is it?
A quadrilateral has all sides equal, its diagonals bisect each other at right angles, and its angles are not 90°. What type of quadrilateral is it?
A quadrilateral has equal diagonals that bisect each other but do not cross at right angles. All its angles are 90°. What type of quadrilateral is it?
A quadrilateral has perpendicular diagonals, exactly one line of symmetry, and no parallel sides. What type of quadrilateral is it?
A quadrilateral has four equal sides, diagonals that are equal in length, and diagonals that bisect each other at 90°. What type of quadrilateral is it?
Select all properties that a square has. (Select all that apply.)
correct_answers: diagonals bisect each other at right angles
all_options: diagonals bisect each other at right angles
exactly one pair of parallel sides
diagonals bisect each other (but not at right angles)

shape_classification_bank.question_text: Select all properties that a rectangle has. (Select all that apply.)
all_options: diagonals bisect each other at right angles
exactly one pair of parallel sides

shape_classification_bank.question_text: Select all properties that a rhombus has. (Select all that apply.)
correct_answers: diagonals bisect each other at right angles
all_options: diagonals bisect each other at right angles

shape_classification_bank.question_text: Select all properties that a parallelogram has. (Select all that apply.)
all_options: diagonals bisect each other at right angles

shape_classification_bank.question_text: Select all properties that a square has. (Select all that apply.)
correct_answers: diagonals bisect the angles they pass through
all_options: diagonals bisect the angles they pass through
exactly one pair of parallel sides
diagonals bisect each other (but not at right angles)

shape_classification_bank.question_text: Select all properties that a rhombus has. (Select all that apply.)
correct_answers: diagonals bisect the angles they pass through
all_options: diagonals bisect the angles they pass through
exactly one pair of parallel sides

shape_classification_bank.question_text: Select all properties that a rectangle has. (Select all that apply.)
correct_answers: diagonals bisect each other (but not at right angles)
all_options: diagonals bisect each other (but not at right angles)
diagonals bisect the angles they pass through
diagonals bisect each other at right angles

shape_classification_bank.question_text: Select all properties that a parallelogram has. (Select all that apply.)
correct_answers: diagonals bisect each other (but not at right angles)
all_options: diagonals bisect each other (but not at right angles)
diagonals bisect each other at right angles

shape_classification_bank.question_text: Select all properties that a rhombus has. (Select all that apply.)
correct_answers: at least one line of symmetry
all_options: at least one line of symmetry
exactly one pair of parallel sides

shape_classification_bank.question_text: Select all properties that a square has. (Select all that apply.)
correct_answers: at least one line of symmetry
diagonals bisect the angles they pass through
all_options: at least one line of symmetry
diagonals bisect the angles they pass through
exactly one pair of parallel sides
diagonals bisect each other (but not at right angles)

cartesian_plane_bank.question_text: Points are plotted: A(2, 3), B(-4, 1), C(5, -2), D(-3, -5). Which point has coordinates (2, 3)?
Points are plotted: A(-1, -6), B(4, 5), C(-3, 2), D(6, -1). Which point has coordinates (4, 5)?
Points are plotted: A(3, -4), B(-2, 5), C(1, 6), D(-5, -3). Which point has coordinates (1, 6)?
Points are plotted: A(-6, 2), B(3, -1), C(-4, -4), D(5, 1). Which point has coordinates (5, 1)?
Points are plotted: A(6, 4), B(-2, -3), C(1, -5), D(-4, 6). Which point has coordinates (6, 4)?
Points are plotted: A(4, -2), B(-3, 5), C(1, 1), D(-6, -4). Which point has coordinates (-3, 5)?
Points are plotted: A(-5, 3), B(2, -6), C(6, 1), D(-1, -2). Which point has coordinates (-5, 3)?
Points are plotted: A(3, 2), B(5, -4), C(-2, 6), D(-6, -1). Which point has coordinates (-2, 6)?
Points are plotted: A(1, -3), B(-4, -5), C(6, 2), D(-1, 4). Which point has coordinates (-1, 4)?
Points are plotted: A(5, 2), B(-3, 4), C(-1, -6), D(4, -3). Which point has coordinates (-1, -6)?
Points are plotted: A(-4, -2), B(6, 3), C(-2, 5), D(1, -4). Which point has coordinates (-4, -2)?
Points are plotted: A(2, -1), B(-5, 6), C(3, 4), D(-6, -5). Which point has coordinates (-6, -5)?
Points are plotted: A(1, 5), B(-2, -3), C(6, -6), D(-4, 2). Which point has coordinates (-2, -3)?
Points are plotted: A(-2, 4), B(3, -5), C(-5, -1), D(6, 2). Which point has coordinates (3, -5)?
Points are plotted: A(4, -1), B(-6, 3), C(-1, -5), D(2, 6). Which point has coordinates (4, -1)?
Points are plotted: A(-3, 2), B(1, 5), C(6, -3), D(-5, -6). Which point has coordinates (6, -3)?
Points are plotted: A(-1, 6), B(-4, -2), C(3, 3), D(5, -4). Which point has coordinates (5, -4)?
Points are plotted: A(2, -6), B(-3, 1), C(4, 4), D(-6, -3). Which point has coordinates (2, -6)?
Points are plotted: A(0, 4), B(-3, -2), C(5, 0), D(-1, 3). Which point has coordinates (5, 0)?
Points are plotted: A(3, -4), B(0, 6), C(-5, 1), D(4, -2). Which point has coordinates (0, 6)?

graph_type_bank.question_text: A teacher records the number of students in each Year 7 class who chose AFL, soccer, netball or basketball as their preferred sport. Which type of graph is most appropriate to display this data?
A school records the total number of library books borrowed by each year level during Term 1. Which type of graph is most appropriate to display this data?
A canteen manager compares the number of pies, sushi rolls, sandwiches and wraps sold on a particular day. Which type of graph is most appropriate to display this data?
Students surveyed 5 different suburbs to find how many households own a pet. They want to compare the counts across suburbs. Which type of graph is most appropriate?
A student records the daily maximum temperature in Melbourne for 14 consecutive days. Which type of graph is most appropriate to display this data?
A class measures the height of a bean plant every day for 3 weeks. Which type of graph is most appropriate to show how the height changed over time?
The Bureau of Meteorology provides monthly rainfall totals for a town over one year. Which type of graph best shows the trend across the 12 months?
A student records their resting heart rate each morning for 10 days. Which type of graph is most appropriate to display this data?
A shop tracks the number of ice creams sold each week from October to March. Which type of graph best shows how sales changed over time?
A Year 8 class surveys students on their favourite music genre. They want to show what percentage of the class chose each genre. Which type of graph is most appropriate?
A student council has a $500 budget. They want to display how the budget is divided among decorations, food, music and prizes. Which type of graph is most appropriate?
A school reports that 40% of students walk, 30% take the bus, 20% are driven and 10% ride a bike. Which type of graph best shows these proportions?
A survey asks Year 7 students which language they speak at home. The results need to show each language as a share of the whole class. Which type of graph is most appropriate?
A teacher wants to investigate the relationship between hours of study and test marks for 30 students. Which type of graph is most appropriate?
A PE teacher records the height and standing long jump distance of 25 students. Which type of graph best shows whether there is a relationship between the two variables?
A science class measures the arm span and height of each student to investigate if they are related. Which type of graph is most appropriate?
A student records the daily temperature and the number of visitors to a local swimming pool over 20 days. Which type of graph best shows the relationship?
A PE teacher measures the 100-metre sprint times of 80 Year 9 students and wants to show how the times are distributed across intervals. Which type of graph is most appropriate?
A council surveys 200 households about weekly water usage in litres. They want to group the data into intervals and show the frequency of each group. Which type of graph is most appropriate?
A school nurse weighs 120 students and wants to display the distribution of weights grouped in 5 kg intervals. Which type of graph is most appropriate?
A teacher collects the test scores (out of 100) of 150 Year 8 students and wants to show how many scored in each 10-mark range. Which type of graph is most appropriate?

A class of 18 students recorded their maths test scores: 45, 52, 58, 61, 63, 67, 70, 72, 74, 75, 78, 80, 82, 85, 88, 91, 93, 97. The teacher wants a display that shows every individual score and allows the median to be found easily. Which type of graph is most appropriate?
A group of 15 students recorded how many minutes they spent reading last night. The teacher wants to display all the raw values and find the mode easily. Which type of graph is most appropriate?

A science class of 20 students measured the length of leaves in millimetres. They want to display every measurement while also showing the shape of the distribution. Which type of graph is most appropriate?
Twelve students recorded the number of push-ups they completed. The teacher wants to display all individual results and quickly identify the range. Which type of graph is most appropriate?

sample_space_bank.question_text: A fair coin is flipped once. How many outcomes are in the sample space?
A standard 6-sided die is rolled once. How many outcomes are in the sample space?
A 4-sided die (tetrahedron) is rolled once. How many outcomes are in the sample space?
An 8-sided die is rolled once. How many outcomes are in the sample space?
A 12-sided die is rolled once. How many outcomes are in the sample space?
Two fair coins are flipped at the same time. How many outcomes are in the sample space?
A 20-cent coin and a 50-cent coin are each flipped once. How many outcomes are in the sample space?
A fair coin is flipped and a 6-sided die is rolled. How many outcomes are in the sample space?
A fair coin is flipped and a 4-sided die is rolled. How many outcomes are in the sample space?
Two standard 6-sided dice are rolled. How many outcomes are in the sample space?
A red 6-sided die and a blue 6-sided die are each rolled once. How many outcomes are in the sample space?
Two 4-sided dice are rolled. How many outcomes are in the sample space?
One card is drawn at random from a standard 52-card deck. How many outcomes are in the sample space?
A spinner has 5 equal sections numbered 1 to 5. The spinner is spun once. How many outcomes are in the sample space?
A spinner is divided into 3 equal sections coloured red, blue and green. The spinner is spun once. How many outcomes are in the sample space?
A spinner has 8 equal sections. It is spun once. How many outcomes are in the sample space?
A bag contains 3 red balls, 4 blue balls and 5 green balls. One ball is drawn at random. How many outcomes are in the sample space?
A bag contains 6 red marbles and 4 yellow marbles. One marble is drawn at random. How many outcomes are in the sample space?
Three fair coins are flipped at the same time. How many outcomes are in the sample space?
A spinner with 4 equal sections is spun and a coin is flipped. How many outcomes are in the sample space?

composite_area_bank.question_text: An L-shaped figure is formed from a 10 cm × 8 cm rectangle with a 3 cm × 4 cm rectangular cutout removed from one corner. What is the area of the L-shape?
An L-shaped figure is formed from a 12 cm × 6 cm rectangle with a 4 cm × 3 cm rectangular cutout removed from one corner. What is the area of the L-shape?
An L-shaped figure is formed from a 15 cm × 10 cm rectangle with a 5 cm × 6 cm rectangular cutout removed from one corner. What is the area of the L-shape?
An L-shaped figure is formed from a 9 cm × 7 cm rectangle with a 2 cm × 3 cm rectangular cutout removed from one corner. What is the area of the L-shape?
An L-shaped figure is formed from a 14 cm × 8 cm rectangle with a 6 cm × 4 cm rectangular cutout removed from one corner. What is the area of the L-shape?
An L-shaped figure is formed from an 11 cm × 9 cm rectangle with a 5 cm × 4 cm rectangular cutout removed from one corner. What is the area of the L-shape?
An L-shaped figure is formed from a 16 cm × 10 cm rectangle with a 7 cm × 5 cm rectangular cutout removed from one corner. What is the area of the L-shape?
An L-shaped figure is formed from an 8 cm × 6 cm rectangle with a 3 cm × 2 cm rectangular cutout removed from one corner. What is the area of the L-shape?
A composite shape is made from a 10 cm × 6 cm rectangle with a right triangle attached to one of the 6 cm sides. The triangle has a base of 6 cm and a height of 4 cm. What is the total area?
A composite shape is made from an 8 cm × 5 cm rectangle with a right triangle attached to one of the 5 cm sides. The triangle has a base of 5 cm and a height of 6 cm. What is the total area?
A composite shape is made from a 12 cm × 7 cm rectangle with a right triangle attached to one of the 7 cm sides. The triangle has a base of 7 cm and a height of 4 cm. What is the total area?
A composite shape is made from a 9 cm × 4 cm rectangle with a right triangle attached to one of the 4 cm sides. The triangle has a base of 4 cm and a height of 6 cm. What is the total area?
A composite shape is made from a 6 cm × 5 cm rectangle with a right triangle attached to one of the 5 cm sides. The triangle has a base of 5 cm and a height of 8 cm. What is the total area?
A composite shape is made from an 11 cm × 8 cm rectangle with a right triangle attached to one of the 8 cm sides. The triangle has a base of 8 cm and a height of 3 cm. What is the total area?
A composite shape is made from a 7 cm × 10 cm rectangle with a right triangle attached to one of the 7 cm sides. The triangle has a base of 7 cm and a height of 6 cm. What is the total area?
A composite shape is made from a 10 cm × 6 cm rectangle with a semicircle attached to one of the 6 cm sides. The diameter of the semicircle is 6 cm. Using π ≈ 3.14, what is the total area?
A composite shape is made from an 8 cm × 4 cm rectangle with a semicircle attached to one of the 4 cm sides. The diameter of the semicircle is 4 cm. Using π ≈ 3.14, what is the total area?
A composite shape is made from a 12 cm × 10 cm rectangle with a semicircle attached to one of the 10 cm sides. The diameter of the semicircle is 10 cm. Using π ≈ 3.14, what is the total area?
A composite shape is made from a 14 cm × 8 cm rectangle with a semicircle attached to one of the 8 cm sides. The diameter of the semicircle is 8 cm. Using π ≈ 3.14, what is the total area?
A composite shape is made from a 6 cm × 4 cm rectangle with a semicircle attached to one of the 4 cm sides. The diameter of the semicircle is 4 cm. Using π ≈ 3.14, what is the total area?
A shape is made from two rectangles joined along one side. The first rectangle is 8 cm × 5 cm. The second rectangle is 6 cm × 3 cm. What is the total area of the shape?
A shape is made from two rectangles joined along one side. The first rectangle is 10 cm × 4 cm. The second rectangle is 7 cm × 6 cm. What is the total area of the shape?
A shape is made from two rectangles joined along one side. The first rectangle is 12 cm × 5 cm. The second rectangle is 8 cm × 3 cm. What is the total area of the shape?
A shape is made from two rectangles joined along one side. The first rectangle is 9 cm × 7 cm. The second rectangle is 5 cm × 4 cm. What is the total area of the shape?
A shape is made from two rectangles joined along one side. The first rectangle is 11 cm × 6 cm. The second rectangle is 4 cm × 9 cm. What is the total area of the shape?

time_zone_bank.question_text: It is 8:00 PM in Sydney (AEST). What time is it in London (GMT)?
It is 3:00 PM in Sydney (AEST). What time is it in Dubai (GST)?
It is 6:00 PM in Sydney (AEST). What time is it in Singapore (SGT)?
It is 10:00 AM in Sydney (AEST). What time is it in New York (EST)?
It is 7:00 PM in Sydney (AEST). What time is it in Los Angeles (PST)?
It is 9:00 AM in London (GMT). What time is it in Sydney (AEST)?
It is 2:00 PM in Dubai (GST). What time is it in Sydney (AEST)?
It is 11:00 AM in Singapore (SGT). What time is it in Sydney (AEST)?
It is 8:00 PM in New York (EST). What time is it in Sydney (AEST)?
It is 5:00 PM in Sydney (AEST). What time is it in UTC?
It is 2:00 PM in London (GMT). What time is it in Dubai (GST)?
It is 7:00 AM in London (GMT). What time is it in Singapore (SGT)?
It is 11:00 AM in London (GMT). What time is it in New York (EST)?
It is 9:00 PM in Dubai (GST). What time is it in Singapore (SGT)?
It is 6:00 PM in Dubai (GST). What time is it in New York (EST)?
It is 4:00 PM in Singapore (SGT). What time is it in London (GMT)?
It is 9:00 PM in New York (EST). What time is it in Los Angeles (PST)?
It is 10:00 AM in Los Angeles (PST). What time is it in London (GMT)?
It is 2:00 AM in Sydney (AEST). What time is it in New York (EST)?
It is 9:00 PM in London (GMT). What time is it in Singapore (SGT)?
It is 11:00 PM in New York (EST). What time is it in Dubai (GST)?
It is 8:00 PM in Los Angeles (PST). What time is it in Sydney (AEST)?
It is 7:00 PM in Sydney (AEDT, daylight saving). What time is it in London (GMT)?
It is 9:00 AM in Sydney (AEDT, daylight saving). What time is it in New York (EST)?
It is 6:00 AM in London (GMT). What time is it in Sydney (AEDT, daylight saving)?

congruence_condition_bank.question_text: In triangles ABC and DEF: AB = DE, BC = EF, and AC = DF. Which congruence condition proves the triangles are congruent?
Two triangles have all three pairs of corresponding sides equal. Which congruence condition applies?
In triangles PQR and XYZ: PQ = XY = 5 cm, QR = YZ = 8 cm, and PR = XZ = 6 cm. You also know that angle Q = angle Y. Which congruence condition is sufficient to prove the triangles are congruent?
Triangle LMN has sides LM = 7, MN = 10, LN = 12. Triangle RST has sides RS = 7, ST = 10, RT = 12. Which congruence condition proves these triangles are congruent?
Two triangular tiles are compared. The first has sides 3 cm, 4 cm and 5 cm. The second has sides 3 cm, 4 cm and 5 cm. Which congruence condition confirms they are congruent?
In triangles PQR and XYZ: PQ = XY, QR = YZ, and the angle between PQ and QR equals the angle between XY and YZ. Which congruence condition proves the triangles are congruent?
Two triangles share two pairs of equal sides, and the included angle (the angle between those two sides) is equal in both triangles. Which congruence condition applies?
In triangles ABC and DEF: AB = DE = 6 cm, angle A = angle D = 50°, and AC = DF = 9 cm. The 50° angle is between the 6 cm and 9 cm sides. Which congruence condition proves the triangles are congruent?
In triangles KLM and PQR: KL = PQ, the angle at L equals the angle at Q, and LM = QR. Which congruence condition proves the triangles are congruent?
Two triangles each have sides of 8 cm and 11 cm with the angle between these two sides measuring 35° in both. Which congruence condition applies?
In triangles ABC and DEF: angle A = angle D, angle B = angle E, and BC = EF. Which congruence condition proves the triangles are congruent?
Two triangles have two pairs of equal angles and one pair of corresponding sides equal (where the side is not between the two known angles). Which congruence condition applies?
In triangles PQR and XYZ: angle P = angle X = 40°, angle R = angle Z = 75°, and PQ = XY = 10 cm. Which congruence condition proves the triangles are congruent?
Two triangles each have angles of 55° and 80°. The side opposite the 80° angle is 12 cm in both triangles. Which congruence condition applies?
In triangles LMN and RST: angle L = angle R, angle N = angle T, and MN = ST. You also know that angle M = angle S (since the angle sum is 180°). Which congruence condition is the most direct proof?
Two right-angled triangles have equal hypotenuses and one other pair of equal sides. Which congruence condition proves they are congruent?
In right-angled triangles ABC and DEF (right angles at C and F): AB = DE (hypotenuses) and AC = DF. Which congruence condition applies?
Triangle PQR has a right angle at R. Triangle XYZ has a right angle at Z. The hypotenuse PQ = XY = 13 cm, and QR = YZ = 5 cm. Which congruence condition proves these triangles are congruent?
Two right-angled triangles each have a hypotenuse of 15 cm and a shorter side of 9 cm. Both right angles are at the same corresponding vertex. Which congruence condition applies?

In triangles ABC and XYZ: angle B = angle Y = 90°, AC = XZ = 10 cm (hypotenuses), and AB = XY = 6 cm. You can also calculate that BC = YZ = 8 cm. Which congruence condition is most directly applicable?

quadrilateral_properties_bank.question_text: Which quadrilateral has exactly one pair of parallel sides?
Which quadrilateral has all sides equal and all angles equal to 90°?
Which quadrilateral has two pairs of equal adjacent sides but no parallel sides?
Which quadrilateral has all sides equal but its angles are NOT all 90°?
Which quadrilateral has all angles equal to 90° but NOT all sides equal?
Which quadrilateral has diagonals that bisect each other at right angles AND has all sides equal?
Which quadrilateral has diagonals that are equal in length AND all angles equal to 90°?
Which quadrilateral has two pairs of parallel sides AND diagonals that bisect each other (but NOT at right angles)?
Which quadrilateral has perpendicular diagonals AND exactly one line of symmetry?
Which quadrilateral has diagonals that bisect each other at right angles AND diagonals that are equal in length?
Which quadrilateral has all sides equal, diagonals that bisect each other at right angles, but its angles are NOT all 90°?
Which quadrilateral has all angles 90°, diagonals that are equal in length, and diagonals that bisect each other at right angles?
Which quadrilateral has opposite sides equal and parallel, diagonals that are equal in length, and all angles equal to 90°?
Which quadrilateral has two pairs of parallel sides, opposite angles equal, but its diagonals are NOT equal in length and do NOT meet at right angles?
Which quadrilateral has perpendicular diagonals, at least one line of symmetry, but does NOT have two pairs of parallel sides?
Select all quadrilaterals whose diagonals bisect each other. (Select all that apply.)
Select all quadrilaterals that have all sides equal. (Select all that apply.)
Select all quadrilaterals that have all angles equal to 90°. (Select all that apply.)
Select all quadrilaterals whose diagonals are perpendicular to each other. (Select all that apply.)
Select all quadrilaterals whose diagonals are equal in length. (Select all that apply.)
Select all quadrilaterals that have at least one pair of parallel sides. (Select all that apply.)
Select all quadrilaterals that have at least one line of symmetry. (Select all that apply.)
Select all quadrilaterals that have opposite sides equal and parallel. (Select all that apply.)
Select all quadrilaterals where both pairs of opposite angles are equal. (Select all that apply.)
Select all quadrilaterals that have exactly two pairs of parallel sides. (Select all that apply.)

outlier_effect_bank.question_text: A data set is: 12, 14, 15, 16, 18. An outlier of 95 is added to the set. Which statement is true?
outlier_effect_bank.correct_answer: The mean increases significantly; the median increases by only 0.5
wrong_answers: Both the mean and median increases by the same amount
The median changes more than the mean
Neither the mean nor the median changes

experimental_probability_bank.question_text: A coin is flipped 40 times and lands heads 26 times. What is the experimental probability of heads?
A die is rolled 50 times and lands on a 6 exactly 15 times. What is the experimental probability of rolling a 6?
A spinner is spun 100 times and lands on blue 35 times. What is the experimental probability of landing on blue?
A marble is drawn from a bag 20 times (with replacement) and a red marble appears 12 times. What is the experimental probability of drawing a red marble?
A card is drawn from a deck 50 times (with replacement) and a heart appears 20 times. What is the experimental probability of drawing a heart?
A ball is randomly selected from a box 40 times (with replacement) and a green ball appears 14 times. What is the experimental probability of selecting a green ball?
A thumbtack is tossed 25 times and lands point-up 10 times. What is the experimental probability of landing point-up?
A coin is flipped 100 times and lands tails 45 times. What is the experimental probability of tails?
A spinner is spun 50 times and lands on red 30 times. What is the experimental probability of landing on red?
A die is rolled 20 times and an even number appears 8 times. What is the experimental probability of rolling an even number?
A fair coin is flipped 100 times and lands heads 58 times. Which statement is correct?
experimental_probability_bank.correct_answer: The experimental probability of heads is greater than the theoretical probability
wrong_answers: The experimental probability of heads is less than the theoretical probability
The experimental probability of heads equals the theoretical probability
It is impossible to compare experimental and theoretical probability

experimental_probability_bank.question_text: A fair six-sided die is rolled 60 times and a 3 appears 10 times. The theoretical probability of rolling a 3 is 1/6. Which statement is correct?
experimental_probability_bank.correct_answer: The experimental probability equals the theoretical probability
wrong_answers: The experimental probability is greater than the theoretical probability
The experimental probability is less than the theoretical probability
It is impossible to compare experimental and theoretical probability

experimental_probability_bank.question_text: A spinner has 4 equal sections coloured red, blue, green and yellow. It is spun 80 times and lands on red 15 times. Which statement is correct?
experimental_probability_bank.correct_answer: The experimental probability of red is less than the theoretical probability
wrong_answers: The experimental probability of red is greater than the theoretical probability
The experimental probability of red equals the theoretical probability
It is impossible to compare experimental and theoretical probability

experimental_probability_bank.question_text: A fair coin is flipped 50 times and lands tails 25 times. Which statement is correct?
experimental_probability_bank.correct_answer: The experimental probability of tails equals the theoretical probability
wrong_answers: The experimental probability of tails is greater than the theoretical probability
The experimental probability of tails is less than the theoretical probability
It is impossible to compare experimental and theoretical probability

experimental_probability_bank.question_text: A bag contains 5 red and 5 blue marbles. A marble is drawn 40 times (with replacement) and red appears 24 times. Which statement is correct?
experimental_probability_bank.correct_answer: The experimental probability of red is greater than the theoretical probability
wrong_answers: The experimental probability of red is less than the theoretical probability
The experimental probability of red equals the theoretical probability
It is impossible to compare experimental and theoretical probability

experimental_probability_bank.question_text: A fair six-sided die is rolled 30 times and a number less than 3 appears 8 times. The theoretical probability of rolling less than 3 is 1/3. Which statement is correct?
experimental_probability_bank.correct_answer: The experimental probability is less than the theoretical probability
wrong_answers: The experimental probability is greater than the theoretical probability
The experimental probability equals the theoretical probability

experimental_probability_bank.question_text: A spinner has 3 equal sections. It is spun 90 times and lands on section A exactly 30 times. Which statement is correct?
experimental_probability_bank.correct_answer: The experimental probability of section A equals the theoretical probability
wrong_answers: The experimental probability of section A is greater than the theoretical probability
The experimental probability of section A is less than the theoretical probability
It is impossible to compare experimental and theoretical probability

experimental_probability_bank.question_text: A fair coin is flipped 200 times and lands heads 90 times. Which statement is correct?
experimental_probability_bank.correct_answer: The experimental probability of heads is less than the theoretical probability
wrong_answers: The experimental probability of heads is greater than the theoretical probability
The experimental probability of heads equals the theoretical probability
It is impossible to compare experimental and theoretical probability

experimental_probability_bank.question_text: A bag contains 3 red, 3 blue and 3 green balls. A ball is drawn 50 times (with replacement) and green appears 22 times. The theoretical probability of green is 1/3. Which statement is correct?
experimental_probability_bank.correct_answer: The experimental probability of green is greater than the theoretical probability
wrong_answers: The experimental probability of green is less than the theoretical probability
The experimental probability of green equals the theoretical probability
It is impossible to compare experimental and theoretical probability

experimental_probability_bank.question_text: A six-sided die is rolled 120 times and an odd number appears 65 times. The theoretical probability of rolling odd is 1/2. Which statement is correct?
experimental_probability_bank.correct_answer: The experimental probability of odd is greater than the theoretical probability
wrong_answers: The experimental probability of odd is less than the theoretical probability
The experimental probability of odd equals the theoretical probability
It is impossible to compare experimental and theoretical probability
